creator: freebsd@dev.thsi.be
text:
The issue of filesystem violations is caused by the fontconfig library when  xelatex is scanning the texmf font directories. So fontconfig checks XDG_CACHE_HOME, but still writes to the system cache, /var/db/fontconfig on freebsd. That problem can easily be demonstrated by running, as root, "mkdir /tmp/cache; find /var/db/fontconfig -type f -delete; env XDG_CACHE_ROOT=/tmp/cache fc-list; ls /var/db/fontconfig" (one might want to save first, then restore the cache after running that command).

I see 3 ways of fixing this:
1. have the tex packages prepopulate the cache on post installation, this might be tricky to do in practice (simply running fc-cache is not sufficient, from my understanding)
2. fix fontconfig
3. stop building the pdf

It seems unlikely that this is the only packge building pdf with pdflatex, is it? Maybe this problem should be brought to ports@ ?

I'll do 3 in the next iteration of this patch. Setting DOCS_BROKEN=  was a bad idea :)


(In reply to Älven from comment #11)

That seems indeed a bit misleading, so probably?
